name: Show/Hide Rule (Conditional Visibility)
summary: SHOW or HIDE form elements based on boolean, string, or other field values using JSON Forms rules
whenToUse: Showing follow-up questions only when a prerequisite is answered
Hiding irrelevant sections based on prior answers
'Do you have dependents?' → show dependent name fields
Hiding a warning message when conditions are met (HIDE effect)
String value matching — e.g., show fields when answer is 'yes'

elements.label: I accept the terms and conditions

options.help: ⚠ You must accept the terms and conditions before submitting.

notes: Attach a `rule` object to any UI schema element (Control, Layout, Group, etc.)
SHOW: element is hidden by default, shown when condition is TRUE
HIDE: element is visible by default, hidden when condition is TRUE — the INVERSE of SHOW
The `condition.scope` points to the field being evaluated; `condition.schema` is a JSON schema to match
Use `const` for exact value matching (boolean, string, number): `{ const: true }`, `{ const: "yes" }`, `{ const: 42 }`
String matching: `{ const: "yes" }` matches exactly 'yes'; `{ enum: ["yes", "maybe"] }` matches either value
IMPORTANT: Hidden fields can still be `required` — use conditional validation (if/then) to avoid validation errors on hidden fields
A rule can be attached to a layout element to show/hide an entire group of fields at once
Choose SHOW vs HIDE based on default state: use SHOW when hidden-by-default, HIDE when visible-by-default
